messages.content:
You are comparing two analyst responses to determine which better demonstrates knowledge of a specific finding from a longitudinal dataset.

Question asked: How would you assess the severity of the current service degradation?

Finding to evaluate: service-B retry rate elevated

Response A:
I’m unable to locate any relevant documentation in the memory system that describes how to assess the severity of the current service degradation. Consequently, I can’t provide a sourced answer to that question at this time.

Response B:
**Severity Assessment – “Current Service Degradation”**

| Area | What the logs show | Why it matters | Severity rating |
|------|-------------------|----------------|-----------------|
| **Public‑facing APIs (checkout, fraud, search, etc.)** | Error rates stay **≤ 0.13 %** (e.g., `/checkout` 0.12 % on 2024‑01‑19 [**cascading_failure_01_ep_005**]; `/auth` 0.05 % on the same day). CPU, memory and network on the gateways are well below any alarm thresholds (CPU ≈ 35 % – 37 % on 2024‑01‑19 [**cascading_failure_01_ep_005**]). CDN hit‑rate stays at **≈ 94 %** (2024‑01‑19 [**cascading_failure_01_ep_005**]). | The customer‑facing experience is still within SLA limits; no user‑visible outage. | **Low** – normal operation. |
| **Identity‑Platform (auth‑audit) endpoints** | • **/sso_sessions** error ≈ 0.6 % (2026‑01‑16 [**cascading_failure_01_dx_015**]) – up from ~0.5 % a

Which response better demonstrates awareness of this finding? The response may use different terminology. Focus on whether the core insight is present, not exact wording.

Respond with ONLY one word: A, B, or TIE
/no_think
